title: Dolomiaea asbukinii
taxonomy: Dolomiaea asbukinii is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Dolomiaea is part of the subfamily Carduoideae and is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. Dolomiaea asbukinii is a perennial herb that belongs to the tribe Cardueae, which includes other thistle-like plants. The species Dolomiaea asbukinii has been assigned the taxonomic rank of species within the kingdom Plantae. Further classification of Dolomiaea asbukinii within the taxonomic hierarchy is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, subfamily Carduoideae, tribe Cardueae, and genus Dolomiaea.

intro: Dolomiaea asbukinii is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and gastrointestinal issues, due to its reported health benefits. Dolomiaea asbukinii is rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
